title: Spin-force from a Nitrogen-Vacancy ensemble drives a 100 mg levitated resonator
authors: Anshuman Nayak, Daehee Kim, Shilu Tian, Jason Twamley
abstract: The force experienced by a spin in a magnetic field gradient underlies many proposals for hybrid quantum systems. These include schemes for mechanically mediated quantum gates, spin squeezing, searches for exotic forces, and motional superpositions for probing the interface between quantum and gravity. Yet, experimentally observing this spin-force for anything larger than atomic scales has proved challenging. In our work, we demonstrate controllable Center-of-Mass motion of a $128 \rm\: mg$ diamagnetically levitated oscillator due to force from an ensemble of Nitrogen-Vacancy (NV) defects in diamond. We induce coherent motion in the oscillator by periodic optical initialisation of the NV spin states, achieving motional amplitudes exceeding $100 \rm\:nm$. Our results mark a key milestone towards spin-based engineering of motional states deep in the high-mass regime.
summary: The researchers successfully drove a 100 mg levitated mechanical oscillator using the magnetic force generated by an ensemble of NV centers in diamond. By periodically polarizing the spins with a laser, they achieved large, phase-coherent mechanical oscillations. This achievement marks a significant step toward using spin-based engineering to control motion in the high-mass regime.
main_problem: The difficulty of experimentally observing the spin-dependent force (spin-force) in mechanical systems larger than the atomic scale.
main_result: Demonstration of controllable center-of-mass motion in a 128 mg diamagnetically levitated resonator driven by the spin-force from an ensemble of Nitrogen-Vacancy (NV) defects.
method: Periodic optical initialization of NV spin states using a 532 nm laser is used to switch the ensemble between thermal and polarized states, creating a periodic force at the mechanical resonance frequency.
model_or_system: A hybrid system consisting of a 128 mg diamagnetically levitated graphite/diamond oscillator and an ensemble of NV centers in diamond, placed in a strong magnetic field gradient provided by permanent magnets.
key_observables: Center-of-mass (COM) oscillation amplitude, Power Spectral Density (PSD) of the resonator motion, and phase coherence of the oscillation.
important_parameters: Mass scale of 100-128 mg, resonance frequency of 17.6 Hz, magnetic field gradient of ~98 T/m, and motional amplitudes up to 1.5 micrometers in vacuum.
main_assumptions: Assumes the polarization time and T1 relaxation time are much shorter than the mechanical oscillation period.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows the experimental schematic and the mechanism of the spin-force; Figure 2 displays the time trace, averaged waveform, and PSD showing the resonance peak; Figure 3 shows the waterfall plot of PSD with increasing laser power.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the scientific problem of macroscopic spin-force detection, describes the experimental setup of the levitated diamond/graphite resonator, details the optical driving and spin-modeling methods, presents results demonstrating phase-coherent motion and verification of the spin-origin, and discusses future scaling potential.
why_interesting: This work demonstrates a bridge between spin dynamics and macroscopic mechanics, relevant to researchers in quantum optics and open quantum systems interested in hybrid quantum systems and the interface of quantum mechanics and gravity.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
